Dummy.notes: Has exactly the same ASM code as Nop.

End.description: Exits script execution and returns control to the player

Wait.description: Pauses for some frames, tracks the timer in a script variable

LoadByte.description: Loads 8-bit value into the specified script register

LoadWord.description: Loads 32-bit value into the specified script register

LoadByteFromAddr.description: Reads byte at a specific memory address into a script register Because scripts are compiled in a code-agnostic fashion, this command can never be assembled.

WriteByteToAddr.description: Writes byte to a specific memory address from a script register Because scripts are compiled in a code-agnostic fashion, this command can never be assembled.

SetPtrByte.description: Writes byte literal to a specific memory address Because scripts are compiled in a code-agnostic fashion, this command can never be assembled.

CopyLocal.description: Copies a byte between script registers

CopyByte.description: Copies a byte between two memory addresses Because scripts are compiled in a code-agnostic fashion, this command can never be assembled.

CompareLocalToLocal.description: Compares values between script reg and script reg

CompareLocalToValue.description: Compares script reg to byte literal

CompareLocalToAddr.description: Compares script reg to memory address Because scripts are compiled in a code-agnostic fashion, this command can never be assembled.

CompareAddrToLocal.description: Compares memory address to script reg Because scripts are compiled in a code-agnostic fashion, this command can never be assembled.

CompareAddrToValue.description: Compares memory address to byte literal Because scripts are compiled in a code-agnostic fashion, this command can never be assembled.

CompareAddrToAddr.description: Compares bytes at two memory addresses Because scripts are compiled in a code-agnostic fashion, this command can never be assembled.

RunScript.description: Creates a new script context to run the indicated script asynchronously
RunScript.notes: Somehow related with parallel common script execution.

CallStd.description: Creates a new script context to run the indicated script and wait
CallStd.notes: The Common Script table is located at 0xFA48C in IPKS arm9.bin, ordered from highest to lowest with structure: u16 Global Script ID | u16 Script file | u16 Text file. The executed script inside script file is determined as: used Global Script ID - tabulated Global Script ID.

GoTo.description: Absolute branch to offset in script
GoTo.notes: Relative Jump can have negative values (backward jump).

ObjectGoTo.description: Branch to offset in script if interacting with a specific object

BGGoTo.description: Branch to offset in script if interacting with a specific bg event
BGGoTo.notes: Maybe jumps to a function if invoking event has a specific ID.

DirectionGoTo.description: Branch to offset in script if facing a specific direction
DirectionGoTo.notes: Supposed. Needs to be tested in game.

Call.notes: Can be used to make loops when calling the own invoking function.

GoToIf.description: Conditional branch to offset in script
GoToIf.notes: Ask AdAstra for the condition list. Condition table is updated after a IfVarValue, IfVarVar, CheckFlag...

CheckFlag.description: Check event flag, store result on context
CheckFlag.notes: It updates the internal condition flag and maybe part of the condition table.

SetFlagVar.description: Set event flag referenced in var

ClearFlagVar.description: Clear event flag referenced in var

CheckFlagVar.description: Check event flag referenced in var, store result in other var

SetTrainerFlag.notes: So this trainer will not battle against you. Used in every Gym after the leader is beaten.

ClearTrainerFlag.notes: So this trainer can be challenged again.

CheckTrainerFlag.description: Check trainer defeated flag, store result on context
CheckTrainerFlag.notes: It updates the internal condition flag and maybe part of the condition table.

SetOrCopyVar.description: Set var from other var or short literal

NonNPCMsg.description: Print non_npc_msg in field window from current map message bank

NonNPCMsgVar.description: Not sure what to call this but it is also a message printer
NonNPCMsgVar.notes: Unlike 002D, this command also accepts a variable as the input field.

WaitABPress.description: Waits for A or B button
WaitABPress.notes: Somehow it uses ScrReg 0 as countdown.

WaitButton.description: Waits for A, B, or dpad. On pressing dpad, does a turn frame.
WaitButton.notes: It seems only A, B, X and direction pad buttons work.

WaitButtonOrDpad.description: Waits for A, B, or dpad. No turn frame.
WaitButtonOrDpad.notes: Supposed. Needs to be tested in game.

CloseMsg.notes: SDSME name is misleading, for no button is needed to be pressed.

HoldMsg.description: Like closemsg but does not clear the graphics.
HoldMsg.notes: Probably keeps the message box alive after a script. ASM differs with CloseMessage only in a BL instruction.

DirectionSignpost.description: Prepares an icon board message box displaying Text Slot message, of Type type, and with Icon image if chosen type supports it
DirectionSignpost.notes: Type values from 0 to 3 are: brown (with icon), green (with icon), gray and blue.

ScrCmd_055.description: Prepares a text board message box of Type type, and with Icon image if chosen type supports it
ScrCmd_055.notes: Type values from 0 to 3 are: brown (with icon), green (with icon), gray and blue.

ScrCmd_057.description: Triggers different Process processes for the board
ScrCmd_057.notes: Process value 0 keeps the board, value 1 creates the board, value 2 hides the board, value 3 shows the board and value 4 deletes the board.

ScrCmd_058.description: Waits the board last process to end

TrainerTips.description: Displays Text Slot message in the current text box, stores the current text box status in Variable

ScrCmd_060.description: Returns 1 to Variable if the player has pressed a button so the board must close, 0 otherwise
ScrCmd_060.notes: Variable is stored in ScrReg 0. Command waits for a button to be pressed and stores result in Variable.

YesNo.description: Opens a Yes-No dialog and stores user response in Variable

ScrCmd_064.description: Prepares a multiple selection menu at X and Y screen position, with Cursor default selected option and possibility of cancelling depending on Cancel, with text bank #321, returns selected slot ID in Selection
ScrCmd_064.notes: If Cancel is 1, the user can select the last option by pressing B button.

ScrCmd_065.description: Prepares a multiple selection menu at X and Y screen position, with Cursor default selected option and possibility of cancelling depending on Cancel, with local texts, returns selected slot ID in Selection
ScrCmd_065.notes: If Cancel is 1, the user can select the last option by pressing B button.

ScrCmd_066.description: Adds an option with Multi Slot ID showing Text Slot text

ScrCmd_067.description: Shows the prepared multi in screen
ScrCmd_067.notes: The game will hide it when the player has selected an option.

ScrCmd_068.description: Prepares a multiple selection list at X and Y screen position, with Cursor default selected option and possibility of cancelling depending on Cancel, with text bank #321, returns selected slot ID in Selection
ScrCmd_068.notes: If Cancel is 1, the user can select the last option by pressing B button. The difference between a Multi and a List seems to be that lists are scrollable.

ScrCmd_069.description: Prepares a multiple selection list at X and Y screen position, with Cursor default selected option and possibility of cancelling depending on Cancel, with local texts, returns selected slot ID in Selection
ScrCmd_069.notes: If Cancel is 1, the user can select the last option by pressing B button. The difference between a Multi and a List seems to be that lists are scrollable.

ScrCmd_070.description: Adds a new option to the list, with List Slot ID and showing text line Option MSG. It also displays text line Highlight MSG when that option is highlighted.
ScrCmd_070.notes:
If Highlight MSG is set to 255 [0xFF], no message will be displayed when the option is highlighted.
This command relies on an open message box in order to display the highlight messages. 

Trying to highlight an option with Highlight MSG different than 255 [0xFF] will result in a crash if no message box is already being displayed.

ScrCmd_071.description: Shows the prepared list in screen
ScrCmd_071.notes: The game will hide it when the player has selected an option.

ScrCmd_072.description: Splits the multi window in Columns different columns

PlayCry.notes:
Types:
0 = Normal Playback
1 = Reduced playback in a double battle
2 = ?
3 = Howl?
4 = Hyper Voice?
5 = Defeated Pokemon
6 = Hyper Voice?
7 = Roar?
8 = Roar?
9 = ?
10 = ?
11 = Statused (e.g. sick Miltank on Route 39)
12 = Reduced playback in a double battle when the Pokemon is statused

WaitCry.description: Waits the current cry to finish

PlayFanfare.description: Pauses current music, then Plays Sound

WaitFanfare.description: Waits for Sound to finish, then resumes music

StopBGM.notes: Parameter is ignored because the game identifies what music is playing.

FadeOutBGM.description: Fade out the music to the target level in the designated number of frames

FadeInBGM.description: Fades in the current music to normal volume in Time frames

ScrCmd_088.notes: Seems to set a flag related to the music system.

ChatotHasCry.description: Checks if Chatot has custom sound, stores answer in Variable
ChatotHasCry.notes: Returns 0 or 1 to the specified Variable. Not for a specific Chatot but for all (they share a custom sound).

ChatotStartRecording.description: Starts Chatot microphone record, stores answer in Variable
ChatotStartRecording.notes: Returns 0 or 1 to the specified Variable depending on successful recording.

ChatotSaveRecording.notes: Maybe saves the custom Chatot sound in the savegame.

ApplyMovement.description: Applies movement at Relative Jump to Event ID
ApplyMovement.notes: Movements must be halfword-aligned in the script file. If you're using an old tool which doesn't align them, force the alignment with a TextPlayerName 0x0 or some unnoticeable command of 3, 5, 7 or 9 bytes length.

WaitMovement.description: Waits for all movements to finish

LockAll.description: Locks every event in the map

ReleaseAll.description: Releases every event in the map

ShowPerson.description: Add Event ID overworld in the map
ShowPerson.notes: It is probable that, if executing this having Event ID overworld already in the map, it gets duplicated.

HidePerson.description: Removes Event ID overworld from the map

ScrCmd_102.description: Locks the camera at position X and Y
ScrCmd_102.notes: Seems to place a invisible NPC at coords X and Y. It appears that both must be the current player position.

FacePlayer.description: Makes the invoking event look at the player
FacePlayer.notes: In fact it simply takes the current player direction and makes the NPC look at the opposite direction.

GetPlayerCoords.description: Saves the current player position in X and Y

GetPersonCoords.description: Saves the current Event ID position in X and Y
GetPersonCoords.notes: If Event ID is deleted (via RemoveOW) it gives 0xFF for both coordinates.

ScrCmd_107.notes: Maybe teleports the player to other coords?

ScrCmd_108.description: If Value is 1, prevents Event ID from disappearing when entering on a new map header

ScrCmd_109.description: Changes the Event ID's movement number to Movement

AddMoney.description: Adds Money to the player's money

SubMoneyImmediate.description: Subtracts Money from the player's money

HasEnoughMoneyImmediate.description: Stores 1 into Variable if the player has Money or more money. Stores 0 otherwise.

ShowMoneyBox.description: Shows the money box at X and Y coordinates of the screen
ShowMoneyBox.notes: Each X and Y unit implies 8 pixels. Values of 0 or higher than the screen may glitch the screen.

UpdateMoneyBox.description: Updates the current money in the money box

ScrCmd_116.description: Shows the Currency box at X and Y coordinates of the screen.
ScrCmd_116.notes:
Currency:

0 = Coins
1 = Battle Points
2 = Athlete Points

ScrCmd_117.description: Hides the currently displayed special currency box.

ScrCmd_118.description: Updates the current Currency in the currency box.
ScrCmd_118.notes: Currency types are the same as above [0074].

GetCoinAmount.description: Stores the current casino coin amount in Variable

GiveCoins.description: Adds Coins to the player's casino coins

TakeCoins.description: Subtracts Coins from the player's casino coins

GiveAthletePoints.description: Adds the given Amount to the player's Athlete Points

TakeAthletePoints.description: Subtracts the given Amount from the player's Athlete Points

CheckAthletePoints.description: Stores 1 into Variable if the player has the given Amount of Athlete Points or more. Stores 0 otherwise.

GiveItem.description: Adds Amount of Item item, stores 1 in Variable if the player had less than 1000 - Amount (100 - Amount if TM/HM), stores 0 otherwise
GiveItem.notes: Both Item and Amount are flexible, but if you use CommonScript 0x7F1 after that (as it is meant to be) you must place these two values in 0x8004 and 0x8005 in order to make the script work properly.

TakeItem.description: Subs Amount of Item item, stores 1 in Variable if the player had at least Amount items in the bag, stores 0 otherwise

HasSpaceForItem.description: Stores 1 in Variable if the player has less than 1000 - Amount of Item items (100 - Amount if TM/HM), stores 0 otherwise

HasItem.description: Stores 1 in Variable if the player has at least Amount of Item items in the bag, stores 0 otherwise
HasItem.notes: Even if the Amount to check for is 0, the check will return 0 if you don't have the item at all.

ItemIsTMOrHM.description: Stores 1 in Variable if Item is TM or HM, stores 0 otherwise

GetItemPocket.description: Stores the bag pocket of Item in Variable
GetItemPocket.notes:
Items = 0
Medicine = 1
Poke Balls = 2
TMs & HMs = 3
Berries = 4
Mail = 5
Battle Items = 6
Key Items = 7

SetStarterChoice.description: Stores Pokémon as the starting Pokémon in the game save data

GenderMsgBox.description: Displays Male Text or Female Text message depending on player's gender

GetSealQuantity.description: Checks the number of a certain type of Ball Seals that the player has. The Variable is the output of how many Seals you have.

GiveRandomSeal.description: Generates three random types of Ball Seals and gives them to the player (repeats are possible). Each Variable will yield a 0 if the player has no more room for the Seal(s) in the Seal Case.
GiveRandomSeal.notes: Example: House in top right of Olivine City

ScrCmd_136.description: Checks the form of the Pokemon in party slot Party Position and returns the form ID in Variable.

GiveMon.description: Gives Pokémon at level Level, store 1 in Variable if succeed and 0 otherwise

GiveEgg.description: Gives a Pokémon egg received from Location text slot at text bank #281

SetMonMove.description: Overwrites Move Slot of the Pokémon at the specified Party Slot with Move ID.
SetMonMove.notes:
Party slot and move slot start at 0 rather than 1, so the ranges are 0-5 and 0-3 respectively.
https://bulbapedia.bulbagarden.net/wiki/List_of_moves

MonHasMove.description:
Checks if a Pokémon at the specified Party Slot knows a move with given Move ID.
Stores 1 into Variable if yes, 0 otherwise.
MonHasMove.notes: Example: Headbutt tutor in Ilex Forest (Script File 92, Script 3)

GetPartySlotWithMove.description: Checks if any Pokémon in the party knows Move ID move, stores in Variable the position of the first Pokémon that knows it or stores 6 if no Pokémon in the party knows the move

GetPhoneBookRematch.description: Stores 1 in Var if rematch is active, stores 0 otherwise.
GetPhoneBookRematch.notes: Gets used when deciding when to let Gym Leaders appear in the overworld for a photo shoot after they are defeated in the Dojo, in the Dojo itself, etc.

NameRival.description: Opens the keyboard for naming the rival, stores 1 in Variable if user cancels the proccess

GetFriendSprite.description:
Stores 97 (Lyra sprite ID) into the Variable if you're playing as Ethan;
Stores 0 (Ethan sprite ID) into the Variable if you're playing as Lyra.
GetFriendSprite.notes: Remember that Overworld Entities with sprite IDs 101 to 116 refer to the value of vars 0x4020-0x402F for the sprite to display.

RegisterPokegearCard.description: Adds the worldmap to the Pokegear if Module is 1, adds the radio if Module is 2

CheckRegisteredPhoneNumber.description: Stores 0 into Variable if Phone Number has not been registered, and 1 if it has been registered.
CheckRegisteredPhoneNumber.notes: Example: Sabrina in Olivine Harbor (Script File 153, Script 7). Used frequently by Gym Leaders appearing in the overworld.

HOFCredits.description: If Location is 0, Hall of Fame and Credits sequence plays -- used for Indigo Plateau. If Location is 1, only the Credits sequence plays -- used for Mt. Silver battle against Red

ChooseStarter.description: Opens the starter Pokémon selection screen

GetEyeTrainerNum.description: For when one or more trainers see you

NamePlayer.description: Opens the keyboard for naming the player, stores 1 in Variable if user cancels the proccess

NicknameInput.description: Opens the keyboard for naming a Pokémon in Party Position, stores 1 in Variable if user cancels the proccess

FadeScreen.description: Fades screen to chosen 15-bit Color, using the chosen Fade Style. The transition will render Frame Count images, of Duration length each.
FadeScreen.notes: Type 0 for fade in, Type 1 for fade out. Color has 15-bit format. Transition is the number of color changes that the game does in the fading.

WaitFade.description: Waits for the screen fade to end

Warp.description: Warps to X and Y position of Map header with initial Dir direction, using Door warp
Warp.notes: When Door is 0 it doesn't use any door animation.

RockClimb.description: Uses rock climb with the Pokémon at Party Position position

Surf.description: Uses surf with the Pokémon at Party Position position

Waterfall.description: Uses waterfall with the Pokémon at Party Position position

ScrCmd_180.description: Flies to Map header map, to coordinates X and Y

FlashEffect.description: Sets weather to 12 (darkness after flash) and then updates the weather graphics

Whirlpool.description: Uses whirlpool with the Pokémon at Party Position position

ScrCmd_183.description: Displays the Hidden Machine summon animation with the Pokémon at Party Position position

PlayerOnBikeCheck.description: Stores 1 in Variable if player is riding the bike, stores 0 otherwise

PlayerOnBikeSet.description: Changes player form from normal to riding the bike if Action is 1, otherwise it sets the normal form to the player

SetBikeStateLock.description: If `lock` is 1, the player's bike state will be locked, meaning they cannot mount or dismount their bike; otherwise, if `lock` is any other value, the bike state will be unlocked. The main use for this command was to block the player from dismounting their bike while they were on Cycling Road.

GetPlayerState.description: Get the player's overworld state. Used in some places to determine whether the player is masquerading as a member of Team Rocket.
GetPlayerState.notes: Values 0 is for normal form, value 1 for riding the bike, value 2 for surfing,  value 3 for Rocket clothing

SetAvatarBits.description: Activates the Form bit in the internal player form data
SetAvatarBits.notes: The purpose of the different bits still needs to be researched.

UpdateAvatarState.description: Updates changes in the internal player form data in the screen

BufferPlayersName.description: In the buffer_XXX commands, the maximum acceptable value for the slot arg is 7. This sets the message format placeholder to whatever is buffered.

BufferRivalsName.description: If you use this before naming your rival, this will be an empty string.

BufferFriendsName.description: Stores counterpart's name in String Buffer

BufferMonSpeciesName.description: For these commands, the additional argument is a flex arg, meaning it can be a literal or a variable.

BufferItemName.description: Stores Item's name in String Buffer

BufferPocketName.description: Stores Pocket's name in String Buffer

BufferTMHMMoveName.description: Stores the name of the move contained in Item TM/HM in String Buffer

BufferMoveName.description: Stores battle Move's name in String Buffer

BufferInt.description: Stores a Number in String Buffer

BufferPartyMonNick.description: Stores the nickname of the Pokémon in Party Position in String Buffer

BufferPlayerUnionAvatarClassName.description: Stores player's Union Room trainer type in String Buffer

BufferSpeciesName.description: The other two args here are unused
BufferSpeciesName.notes: The two Unused parameters are for defining male/female nouns and singular/plural nouns respectively. However both don't apply in this command.

BufferStarterSpeciesName.description: Stores the name of the player' starter Pokémon in String Buffer

BufferDPPtRivalStarterSpeciesName.description: These two commands are leftover from Platinum. As such, they are configured to check for the Sinnoh starters instead of the Johto starters. Since the player will never start with any of Turtwig, Chimchar, or Piplup, this command will always return TURTWIG, and the following CHIMCHAR. These commands are unused in HGSS.

BufferDPPtFriendStarterSpeciesName.description: Stores the name of the counterpart' starter Pokémon in String Buffer

GetStarterChoice.description: Gets your starter species into the indicated variable. Used to calculate which rival party to use.

BufferDecorationName.description: Loads an empty string in String Buffer

BufferMapSecName.description: Stores Map header name in String Buffer

ScrCmd_211.description: Selects a zone depending on a random seed, then stores the zone ID in Map and the corresponding swarm Pokémon of that zone in Pokémon

GetTrainerNum.description: For when you talk to a trainer
GetTrainerNum.notes: This means you shouldn't use this command in a normal script.

TrainerBattle.description: Starts a battle against Trainer 1 and, if Trainer 2 is neither 0 nor the same as Trainer 1, a double battle against both. Continues without warping to Pokemon Center/home if Result is equal to 1.
TrainerBattle.notes: Your party will also be fully healed after the battle if Result is 1. Example: Cherrygrove City fight with Rival

TrainerMessage.description: Displays the text of Trainer ID trainer at condition Type
TrainerMessage.notes: The possible Type values are the following (expand the note for see them)

GetTrainerMsgParams.description: If the script ID corresponds to a single battle trainer, stores 0 in Noticing, 2 in Defeat and 0 in One Pokémon. If it corresponds to a double battle, stores 3, 5 and 6 or 7, 9 and 10 depending on whether it's the first or the second trainer
GetTrainerMsgParams.notes: This means you shouldn't use this command in a normal script.

GetRematchMsgParams.description: If the script ID corresponds to a single battle trainer, stores 17 in Noticing, 0 in Defeat and 0 in One Pokémon. If it corresponds to a double battle, stores 18, 0 and 6 or 19, 0 and 10 depending on whether it's the first or the second trainer
GetRematchMsgParams.notes: This means you shouldn't use this command in a normal script.

TrainerIsDoubleBattle.description: Stores 0 in Variable if the script ID corresponds to a single battle, stores 1 if it corresponds to a double battle
TrainerIsDoubleBattle.notes: This means you shouldn't use this command in a normal script.

EncounterMusic.notes: It checks if player is in Johto or Kanto for that.

WhiteOut.description: Return to Pokémon Center or player's house after being defeated

StaticWildWonOrCaughtCheck.description: Stores 0 in Variable if player defeated or catched the wild Pokemon, stores 1 otherwise

PartyCheckForDouble.description: Stores 1 in Variable if player has at least 2 not fainted Pokémon, stores 0 otherwise

ScrCmd_223.description: Starts a battle against trainer with ID 1

ScrCmd_224.description: Sets the trainer flag with the same ID as the invoking event

GoToIfTrainerDefeated.description: Jumps to a function, Relative Jump bytes forward or backward, if trainer flag with the invoking event ID is set

PartyHasPokerus.description: Stores 1 in Variable if one or more Pokémon in the party are infected with the Pokérus

MonGetGender.description: Stores 0 in Variable if Pokémon at Party Position is male, 1 if is female and 2 if gender is unknown
MonGetGender.notes: Unknown gender value is supposed and should be checked.

GetDynamicWarpFloorNo.description: Stores in Variable the current floor in a elevator

ElevatorCurFloorBox.description: Shows the current floor number in a box at X and Y coordinates in the screen
ElevatorCurFloorBox.notes: Each X and Y unit implies 8 pixels. Values of 0 or higher than the screen may glitch the screen. Set Variable to 0xFFFF to remove the window when you're done.

CountJohtoDexSeen.description: Stores in Variable the total seen Pokemon in the Johto Pokédex

CountJohtoDexOwned.description: Stores in Variable the total obtained Pokemon in the Johto Pokédex

CountNationalDexSeen.description: Stores in Variable the total seen Pokemon in the National Pokédex

CountNationalDexOwned.description: Stores in Variable the total obtained Pokemon in the National Pokédex

GetDexEvalResult.description: The actual Pokedex evaluation. national: 0 = Johto dex, 1 = National dex msg: Variable to return the message ID fanfare: Variable to return the fanfare ID

RocketTrapBattle.description: Starts a wild battle against a Pokémon of level Level

ScrCmd_250.description: Starts a first wild battle against a Pokémon of level Level
ScrCmd_250.notes: The difference between this and other similar commands is that Bag, Run and Pokémon buttons are disabled.

GetSaveFileState.description: Stores 0 in Variable if a different savegame exists and it's not possible to save, stores 1 if no previous savegame exists, stores 2 if game has to save a lot of data and stores 3 if game can perform a quick save

MartBuy.description: Opens the common mart shopping screen

SpecialMartBuy.description: Opens the Mart ID mart shopping screen
SpecialMartBuy.notes: Used in the Departament Store for a shop with specific fixed items. At 0x48188 of arm9.bin (IPKS) is the pointer to the shops table, each entry of the table being a pointer to an item table.

DecorationMart.description: Opens the Mart ID goods mart shopping screen

SealMart.description: Opens the Mart ID seal mart shopping screen

OverworldWhiteOut.description: Used with poison faint, but due to overworld poison mechanics in gen 4, this is never reached.

SetSpawn.description: Sets the player's last warp as Warp ID. This affects Fly, whiting out, Escape Rope, etc.
SetSpawn.notes:
This would affect actions like Escape Rope use, Game Over, etc.
NONE            =  0
NEW_BARK        =  1
CHERRYGROVE     =  2
VIOLET          =  3
AZALEA          =  4
GOLDENROD       =  5
ECRUTEAK        =  6
OLIVINE         =  7
CIANWOOD        =  8
MAHOGANY        =  9
LAKE_OF_RAGE    = 10
BLACKTHORN      = 11
MT_SILVER       = 12
PALLET          = 13
VIRIDIAN        = 14
PEWTER          = 15
CERULEAN        = 16
LAVENDER        = 17
VERMILION       = 18
CELADON         = 19

CINNABAR        = 21
INDIGO          = 22
SAFFRON         = 23
SAFARI          = 24
FRONTIER        = 25
POKEATHLON      = 26
VICTORY_ROAD    = 27
UNION_CAVE      = 28
MT_MOON         = 29
ROCK_TUNNEL     = 30

GetPlayerGender.description: Are you a boy? Or are you a girl?

HealParty.description: Heals every Pokémon in the player's party
HealParty.notes: That does not include the black fade nor the healing sound.

BufferUnionRoomAvatarChoices.description: Generates the four Union Room Trainer class options based on Trainer ID and gender into String Buffers 0, 1, 2, and 3

UnionRoomAvatarIdxToTrainerClass.description: Returns the Trainer class that the player chose for the Union Room to Variable based on the Choice
UnionRoomAvatarIdxToTrainerClass.notes: The Choice is made through a MultiTouchStandardText with the Trainer class options generated with TextUnionRoomTrainerClassOptions, not through the command itself

ScrCmd_289.description: Sets the player Trainer class sprite for the Union Room as determined by CheckUnionRoomTrainerSprite

CheckPokedex.description: Stores 1 in Variable if player has the Pokédex, stores 0 otherwise

GivePokedex.description: Gives the Pokédex to the player

CheckRunningShoes.description: Stores 1 in Variable if player has the running shoes, stores 0 otherwise

GiveRunningShoes.description: Gives the running shoes to the player

CheckBadge.description: Checks if player has Badge ID badge, stores result in Variable

GiveBadge.description: Gives the player Badge ID badge

CountBadges.description: This command is never used because it is assumed that you cannot get the 8th badge before the other 7, or the 16th before the other 15. This assumption does not account for tweaking.

ScrCmd_297.description: Stores 1 in Variable if flag 0x960 is set, stores 0 otherwise

CheckEscortMode.description: Stores 1 in Variable if the following event flag is activated, stores 0 otherwise

SetEscortMode.description: Activates the following event flag, so you can no longer use the bike, surf, rock climb...

ClearEscortMode.description: Deactivates the following event flag, so you can use the bike, surf, rock climb... again

ScrCmd_305.description: Stores 1 in Variable if flag 0x964 is set, stores 0 otherwise

ScrCmd_307.description: Prepares a building located at Map X and Map Y coordinates in a map located at Matrix X and Matrix Y in the current matrix for animation, with Door ID animation ID

ScrCmd_308.description: Wait animation of building with Door ID animation ID

ScrCmd_309.description: Free animation of building with Door ID animation ID

ScrCmd_310.description: Triggers first animation of building with Door ID animation ID

ScrCmd_311.description: Triggers second animation of building with Door ID animation ID

BufferDaycareMonNicks.description: Stores first daycare Pokémon nickname in string buffer 0, the second daycare Pokémon nickname in string buffer 1 and the first Pokémon trainer's name in buffer 2
BufferDaycareMonNicks.notes: If there's only one Pokémon in the daycare, string buffer 1 does not change. If no Pokémon is in the daycare, commands does nothing.

GetDaycareState.description: If daycare Pokémon have an egg, stores 1 in Variable. Otherwise it stores 0 if no Pokémon is in the daycare, 2 if only one Pokémon is in the daycare or 3 if there are two Pokémon

ScrCmd_315.description: ??? Something to do with the Goldenrod Dept. Store Basement, probably moving around blocks or opening access to doors.

VioletGymInit.description: Initializes Violet Gym internal data and 3D model animations

VioletGymElevator.description: Triggers model #111 to move up or down

GetPartyCount.description: Stores in Variable the current number of Pokémon in the party

ScrCmd_333.description: Opens up the Bag screen with the user only being able to select an item from the specified pocket. WARNING: this command is nonfunctional after opening the Bag -- you will be unable to select anything.

MovePerson.description: Sets Event ID spawn position to global coords X and Y

MovePersonFacing.description: Moves Event ID to global coords X, Y, Z with Dir direction
MovePersonFacing.notes: Even though some maps are higher, the Z parameter is often 0. Game seems to calculate the correct Z position if it is wrong.

SetObjectMovementType.description: Sets Event ID's default move code to Movement ID

LotoIDSearch.description:
Takes a Winning Number as input and responds as follows.
If no matching Pokémon could be found or an error occurs: 
MatchPos: 0; Digits: 0; PC: 0.

For all other cases:

If the matching Pokémon is in the Player's party, PC will be assigned 0.
Otherwise, if it's in the Player's Storage System, PC will be assigned 1.

MatchPos stores the zero-based position of the winning Pokémon (either as Party pos or Storage System pos, depending on the value of PC).

When there's a match, the Digits var is assigned the number of matching digits of the Winning Pokémon's PID.
In case of multiple matches, priority is always given to the one with the most matching digits.
LotoIDSearch.notes:
Supposed by analogy with Platinum.

To retrieve the exact position of a winning Pokémon in the Storage System, first make sure PC equals 1. 

The zero-based PC Page and Position indices can be calculated:

PC Page index = MatchPos / 30
Position in page = MatchPos % 30

where % is the modulo operator.

GetFollowPokePartyIndex.description: Returns the following Pokémon's party slot to Variable. Crashes if there are no alive Pokémon in the party

ScrCmd_728.description: Makes the following Pokémon flash white

ScrCmd_729.description: Checks if player has at least 1 not fainted Pokémon, stores the result in Variable

ScrCmd_730.description: Checks if player has a following Pokémon, stores the result in Variable. Returns False if you have a following Pokémon

ScrCmd_732.description: Increases or decreases following Pokémon mood with a signed 8-bit Integer. Maximum values are 127 and -127

ScrCmd_733.description: Checks if the following Pokémon's type matches Type. Returns True if so

ScrCmd_734.description: Makes the following Pokémon jump in place Jumps number of times

ScrCmd_735.description: Checks if Kurt currently has an Apricorn for turning into a ball. If Result is 1, Kurt has an Apricorn already.
ScrCmd_735.notes: If not 0, script continues to check if daily flag is reset, then gives you the apricorns

ScrCmd_737.description: Gets the ID of the Ball Kurt has made and returns it to Var

GetTotalApricornCount.description: Returns how many total Apricorns you have to Var

TouchscreenMenuHide.description: Changes the low screen design to the green Pokéball background

TouchscreenMenuShow.description: Sets the default low screen design

GetMenuChoice.description: Opens a Yes-No selection, stores player's answer in Variable
GetMenuChoice.notes: Crashes if OpenTouchScreen is not used previously.

MenuInitStdGmm.description: Prepares a multiple selection menu with text bank #191, returns selected slot ID in Selection

MenuInit.description: Prepares a multiple selection menu with local texts, returns selected slot ID in Selection. If Close Status is 1, pressing B will automatically select the last option in the list.

MenuItemAdd.description: Adds a box with Multi Box Slot ID showing Text Slot text on the bottom screen and Top Screen Text on the top in a message box. Text depends on whether you use MultiTouchStandardText or MultiTouchLocalText

RockSmashItemCheck.description: Checks if an item was generated after using Rock Smash. If Following Poke Status is 0, the bonus granted from the following Pokémon being present is not granted. If an item is generated, Result equals TRUE. The item is returned to Item

TryHeadbuttEncounter.description: Tries to generate a Headbutt encounter. If an encounter is generated, Variable is set to TRUE

Cinematic.description:
Renders a cutscene
0 = Ho-Oh appearing from the sky
1 = Lugia bursting out of the waterfall
2 = Arceus stock image cutscene

ShowLegendaryWing.description: Shows the Rainbow Wing (0) or the Silver Wing (1) above the player

GiveTogepiEgg.description: Gives an egg containing a Togepi that knows Extrasensory
GiveTogepiEgg.notes: Effectively a hardcoded version of GivePokemonEgg, but programmed to teach the Togepi Extrasensory.

GiveSpikyEarPichu.description: Gives the player a Pichu in Spiky-Eared form

RadioMusicIsPlaying.description: Stores 1 in Result if the current Seq is playing from the Pokégear radio. Stores 0 otherwise.
RadioMusicIsPlaying.notes:
1 = SEQ_GS_RADIO_MARCH, SEQ_GS_P_RADIO_MARCH
2 = SEQ_GS_RADIO_KOMORIUTA, SEQ_GS_P_RADIO_KOMORIUTA
3 = SEQ_GS_RADIO_R_101
4 = SEQ_GS_RADIO_R_201
5 = SEQ_GS_HUE, SEQ_GS_P_HUE
6 = SEQ_GS_RADIO_UNKNOWN

CasinoGame.notes: In every other version of HGSS, Machine and City are both 0 in the final scripts for both Game Corners

SetFollowMonInhibitState.description:
0 = Following Pokémon allowed
1 = Following Pokémon unallowed

ScriptOverlayCmd.description: If Action is 0, load an overlay from the list in the Notes column. Otherwise, unload the overlay
ScriptOverlayCmd.notes:
0 = Overlay 20 (Size Record)
1 = Overlay 24 (Bug-Catching Contest)
2 = Overlay 21 (Fossils)
3 = Overlay 22 (Special Currency)
4 = Overlay 25 (Trainer House)

BugContestAction.description: If Action is 0, check to initialize the Bug Catching Contest based on Day. Otherwise, end the Bug Catching Contest

BufferBugContestWinner.description: Stores the name of the person that placed in rank Rank. If the player places at rank Rank, the player's name is stored in string buffer 0. If another Trainer places at rank Rank, the Trainer Class and Trainer name are stored in string buffers 2 and 0 respectively. The obtained Pokemon is stored in string buffer 1, and the score is stored in string buffer 3

JudgeBugContest.description: Generates the results of the Bug Catching Contest

BufferBugContestMonNick.description: Stores the name of the captured bug from the Bug Catching Contest in String Buffer. Returns 1 to Variable if the party is already full

BugContestGetTimeLeft.description: Stores remaining time in the Bug Catching Contest in String Buffer

IsBugContestantRegistered.description: Checks if the Participant is participating in the Bug Catching Contest. Returns 1 if true to Variable.
IsBugContestantRegistered.notes:
0 = Bug Catcher Don
1 = Bug Catcher Ed
2 = Ace Trainer Abby
3 = Poke Fan William
4 = Bug Catcher Benny
5 = Camper Barry
6 = Picnicker Cindy
7 = Bug Catcher Josh
8 = Youngster Samuel
9 = School Boy Kipp

ScrCmd_795.description: Shows mom savings money box at X and Y coordinates of the screen

ScrCmd_804.description:
Updates the town map to include Kanto
1 = Normal, Johto-only view
2 = Whole map, Kanto included

MomGiftCheck.description: Checks if there is currently a gift from Mom to be picked up

ScrCmd_814.notes: Used in Cerulean City Gym so that the Gym theme is not overwritten by the Surf music

ScrCmd_815.notes: Updates an unknown part of the FieldSystem data structure

GetPartyMonForm2.description: Checks what form Pokémon at Party Slot position has. The Variable stores the form ID.

ScrCmd_829.description: Checks if the following Pokémon is next to the player in any direction

CheckKyogreGroudonInParty.description: Stores 1 in Variable if player has both Kyogre and Groudon in the Pokémon party and they have been caught in Johto, stores 0 otherwise

BufferItemNameIndef.description: Stores Item's lowercase name in String Buffer

BufferItemNamePlural.description: Stores Item's plural noun in String Buffer

BufferPartyMonSpeciesNameIndef.description: Stores the name of the Pokémon in Party Position in String Buffer with default color tags encoding
BufferPartyMonSpeciesNameIndef.notes: International release exclusive. Names stored at text file 238.

BufferTrainerClassNameIndef.description: Stores Trainer class' lowercase noun in String Buffer

BufferSealNamePlural.description: Stores Seal's plural noun in String Buffer

Capitalize.description: Capitalizes the string currently stored in String Buffer

BufferDeptStoreFloorNo.description: Stores Floor's name in String Buffer
BufferDeptStoreFloorNo.notes: International release exclusive. Names stored at text file 191, lines 0x74 to 0x7A.

WarpOut.description: Makes overworld ascend to the sky. No rotation involved.

WarpIn.description: Makes overworld fall from the sky. No rotation involved.

SetInvisible.description: Makes event invisible. It can still be interacted with and will still have collision.

LockDir.description: Prevents event from changing direction. It can still move, but it will always face the same direction.

UnlockDir.description: Allows event to change direction again.

PauseAnimation.description: Disables event's animation. It can still move, but it will not animate.

EmoteExclamationMark.description: Shows an exclamation mark above the event's head. Used commonly to indicate that the event is surprised or has noticed something.
